item.title: U.S. launches another round of retaliatory strikes on Iran, Pentagon says
item.description: U.S. forces launched their third round of strikes this week on Iran, this time in retaliation for an attack on a commercial ship in the Strait of Hormuz, the Pentagon said.

item.title: This week on "Sunday Morning" (July 12)
item.description: A look at the features for this week's broadcast of the Emmy-winning program, hosted by Jane Pauley.

item.title: Bull gores runner in the face at Spain's San Fermin bull run festival
item.description: A chaotic bull run at Spain's San Fermin festival in Pamplona on Saturday left 13 people injured, including one runner who was pierced by a horn in the face.

item.title: 200 young campers, staff rescued amid record flooding in Missouri
item.description: More than 200 people at Camp Taum Sauk in Missouri, were rescued after 6 to 12 inches of rain fell along the Taum Sauk Reservoir.

item.title: U.S. citizen working in Congo tests positive for Ebola, CDC says
item.description: The Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention said the outbreak is the fastest-growing Ebola outbreak recorded on the continent.

item.title: High energy costs from Iran war heighten pressure on struggling farmers
item.description: As the agriculture industry in Louisiana contends with major energy cost hikes brought on by the Iran war, some farmers are unsure if their businesses will survive.

item.title: Congressman says he was detained by Israeli settlers in the West Bank
item.description: Congressman Ro Khanna said members of the Israel Defense Forces spoke with the settlers and moved a car to block the road.

item.title: Apalachee High School shooting suspect set to change plea, documents show
item.description: Colt Gray is scheduled to appear in Barrow County Superior Court on July 24 for a plea hearing, court documents show.

item.title: Houston man didn't threaten ICE agent before being fatally shot, lawyer for witnesses says
item.description: Eyewitnesses say Lorenzo Salgado Araujo did not threaten ICE agents before he was shot and killed during a vehicle stop, an attorney said, contradicting an agent's account. The Department of Homeland Security said that officers were looking for a different person when they stopped Salgado Araujo's vehicle.

item.title: Exclusive discounts from CBS Mornings Deals
item.description: On this edition of CBS Mornings Deals, we show you items that will help improve your everyday lifestyle. Visit cbsdeals.com to take advantage of these exclusive deals today. CBS earns commissions on purchases made through cbsdeals.com.

item.title: Dave Portnoy on running "Barstool Sports" like "SNL"
item.description: Dave Portnoy founded "Barstool Sports" after quitting a sales job he hated and deciding to strike out on his own.

item.title: Trump warns Iran "missiles are locked and loaded" if they attempt assassination
item.description: President Trump threatened to "decimate and destroy" Iran if they try to assassinate him. This comes as the U.S. and Tehran continue negotiations. Here's what to know about where things stand.

item.title: Will these extreme stadium eats hit a home run?
item.description: Baseball season is in full swing, and millions of fans are dreaming about their team winning -- and their favorite bleacher grub. Here are the most extreme eats and the creative combos that knock it out of the park.

item.title: Nolan Wells' mom speaks out after son found dead: "We just want to know what happened"
item.description: Teen football player Nolan Wells was found dead on a Mississippi island days after he vanished during a July Fourth outing. Wells' parents are searching for answers, saying that they don't believe their son would have stayed behind on the island by choice.

item.title: South African soccer player who competed in the World Cup dies at 25
item.description: Jayden Adams' death was confirmed by South Africa's minister of sport, arts and culture on Saturday.

item.title: Saturday Sessions: The James Hunter Six perform "A Sure Thing"
item.description: James Hunter is a Grammy-nominated R&B artist known for his gritty voice and songwriting acumen. Here's The James Hunter Six with "A Sure Thing."

item.title: Saturday Sessions: The James Hunter Six perform "Gun Shy"
item.description: James Hunter is a Grammy-nominated R&B artist known for his gritty voice and songwriting acumen. Here's The James Hunter Six with "Gun Shy."

item.title: New York Times journalists issued subpoenas over Air Force One reporting
item.description: The subpoenas were issued after the New York Times reported on alleged security concerns with the new Qatari-gifted Air Force One.

item.title: Saturday Sessions: The James Hunter Six perform "Here And Now"
item.description: James Hunter is a Grammy-nominated R&B artist known for his gritty voice and songwriting acumen. Here's The James Hunter Six with "Here and Now."

item.title: Chef Greg Baxtrom serves delicious Midwestern meals to New Yorkers
item.description: Chef Greg Baxtrom is bringing approachable Heartland flavors to New York City with 5 Acres, a Midwestern restaurant in the iconic Rockefeller Center. Baxtrom talks about creativity with vegetables and shares his struggle with sobriety.

item.title: Train lovers seek out the lingering romance of steam locomotives
item.description: Trainspotters love to see a Union Pacific locomotive rounding a curve or crossing a bridge. First built in 1941, these moving museums showcase the best of American railroad history.

item.title: Prince Harry and Meghan meet with King Charles III for first time in over four years
item.description: Prince Harry and Meghan, Duchess of Sussex, visited King Charles and Queen Camilla for the first time in over four years following a week of controversy surrounding security efforts in the United Kingdom.

item.title: Barstool Sports' Dave Portnoy on pizza, provocation and the public eye
item.description: Barstool Sports founder Dave Portnoy discusses his new book, "Cancel Me If You Can."

item.title: College funds, retirement planning, and more: Jill Schlesinger answers your financial questions
item.description: CBS News business analyst Jill Schlesinger is here to help out with your financial dilemmas, from smart investing options to building college funds.

item.title: Hundreds rescued amid historic flash flooding in Missouri
item.description: A deluge of rain flooded roads and homes in Missouri, prompting a state of emergency. Hundreds of people were rescued.

item.title: 7/11: CBS Saturday Morning
item.description: Hundreds were rescued from flooding in Missouri. President Trump threatened to "decimate and destroy" Iran if they try to assassinate him. Plus, Barstool Sports' Dave Portnoy sat down with Kelly O'Grady to discuss his new book.

item.title: Pitbull sets record for largest gathering of people wearing bald caps
item.description: A total of 22,141 fans wore the caps in London's Hyde Park on Friday ahead of his set at the British Summer Time festival.

item.title: 10 killed, including Bahamian band, in small plane crash, officials say
item.description: The names of those on board the plane have not been released by officials but members of Da Pond Band were among the dead, according to a source.

item.title: Bipartisan housing bill becomes law after Trump refuses to sign it
item.description: A landmark housing bill automatically became law overnight after President Trump declined to sign it.

item.title: Curtis Sliwa says "it's time for Bibi to go" after poll finds Jewish Americans favor Mamdani more
item.description: A new poll from The Associated Press and the National Opinion Research Center finds that New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani has a higher favorability rating among Jewish Americans than Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu. Curtis Sliwa, who faced off against Mamdani as the Republican mayoral candidate, joins with his reaction.
